versions.notes: Initial versioned release with auto-update checks
Detect overlapping Claude sessions (warning at startup, note at exit, marker in --stats), add macOS sound support, and use os.tmpdir() for cross-platform temp files (Linux, WSL, macOS).
Add --no-noop and --noop-threshold flags to exclude long idle/typing pauses (e.g. waiting for budget replenishment) from stats.
Pro-rate session times to queried date range. Sessions spanning midnight no longer inflate single-day stats with time from other days.
Show per-day breakdown in multi-day date range stats (e.g. --stats week, --stats DATE1 DATE2).
Detect stalled sessions (e.g. budget limit, network disconnect) after 60 minutes of no PTY output. Timer stops and work is counted only up to the last observed activity.
Add --tasks command: correlate git commit history with session logs to estimate per-task development time. Supports merge-based and linear repos, with --export-md for markdown output.

Fix inflated stats: agent_stop/agent_stall/typing_stall with agent_work_ms=0 now correctly transition phase to idle, preventing overnight sessions from being counted as agent work. Also cap standalone task window lookback to 2 hours.
Resolve cleaned-up worktree sessions to their parent git repo, so work done in worktrees is attributed to the correct project and task windows.

Extend task windows backward using session event data. From each task's first commit, walks back through the session to session_start, trimming at any idle gap >1h. Dramatically improves attribution for work-then-commit patterns.

Detect parallel tasks (overlapping commit windows) and show both per-task effort and wall-clock deduplicated totals. Terminal output marks parallel tasks and shows overlap. Markdown export includes dual tables: per-task effort and wall-clock allocation with parallel tasks combined.

Update checks are now opt-in. On first interactive run, claude-timed asks whether to check for new versions on startup; the answer is saved to settings.json (repo root, or ~/.claude/timings/ if globally installed). Edit that file to change the preference later.
